title: DiDi Shop Growth Intern
skills: Design and execute A/B tests and localized growth experiments to improve shop sign-up conversion and first-week activation, aiming for measurable uplifts in conversion rate (e.g., +5-15% per experiment).
Analyze platform and merchant data to identify high-impact growth levers, producing weekly dashboards and actionable insights that inform product and marketing priorities.
Coordinate end-to-end merchant acquisition campaigns (online and in-field), managing timelines, creative assets, and partner communication to reach monthly acquisition targets.
Support onboarding and enablement processes by creating playbooks, onboarding flows, and FAQ resources that reduce merchant time-to-first-sale and improve activation KPIs.
Collaborate with product managers and engineers to scope and prioritize feature requests based on merchant feedback and data, documenting requirements and success metrics for iterative releases.
Conduct competitive and market research on local commerce trends and merchant pain points to propose new growth initiatives aligned with DiDi’s regional strategies.
Monitor post-onboarding merchant health and retention signals, proposing targeted re-engagement tactics (promotions, training, co-marketing) to lift 30/60/90-day retention rates.
Currently pursuing a degree in Industrial Engineering, Business, Marketing, Economics, Statistics, or a related field is preferred but not mandatory.
Proficiency in data analysis using Excel
SQL, Python, or BI tools such as Tableau/Looke (nice to hacve)
Familiarity with digital marketing channels (SEM, social, in-app push, CRM) and experience running or supporting performance campaigns with measurable KPIs.
Strong communication and stakeholder management skills to work effectively with product, engineering, regional operations, and external merchant partners.
A results-oriented, analytical mindset with attention to detail, demonstrated by prior project work, internships, or coursework that includes measurable outcomes.
Ability to work in a fast-paced, cross-functional environment, prioritize multiple tasks, and adapt plans based on data and business needs.
Cultural sensitivity and local market awareness relevant to DiDi’s operating regions, with the ability to tailor merchant strategies to urban mobility and local commerce dynamics.
description:
About the job

About The Company

DiDi Global Inc. is the world’s leading mobility technology platform. It offers a wide range of app-based services across markets including Asia-Pacific, Latin America and Africa, including ride hailing, taxi hailing, chauffeur, hitch and other forms of shared mobility as well as auto solutions, food delivery, intra-city freight, and financial services.

DiDi provides car owners, drivers, and delivery partners with flexible work and income opportunities. It is committed to collaborating with policymakers, the taxi industry, the automobile industry and the communities to solve the world’s transportation, environmental and employment challenges through the use of AI technology and localized smart transportation innovations. DiDi strives to create better life experiences and greater social value, by building a safe, inclusive and sustainable transportation and local services ecosystem for cities of the future.

For more information, please visit: www.didiglobal.com/news

About The Team/role

As a DiDi Shop Growth Intern, you will support user and merchant strategy efforts for DiDi's in-app ecosystem, directly driving monthly active growth and incremental GMV (gross merchandise value). You will work within the DiDi Shop Growth team to deliver data-driven experiments, localized activation campaigns, and process improvements that increase shop sign-ups, activation rates, and merchant lifetime value, reinforcing DiDi’s strategy to deepen platform monetization and improve user experience across millions of riders and drivers.

In this role, you'll be...


Design and execute A/B tests and localized growth experiments to improve shop sign-up conversion and first-week activation, aiming for measurable uplifts in conversion rate (e.g., +5-15% per experiment).
Analyze platform and merchant data to identify high-impact growth levers, producing weekly dashboards and actionable insights that inform product and marketing priorities.
Coordinate end-to-end merchant acquisition campaigns (online and in-field), managing timelines, creative assets, and partner communication to reach monthly acquisition targets.
Support onboarding and enablement processes by creating playbooks, onboarding flows, and FAQ resources that reduce merchant time-to-first-sale and improve activation KPIs.
Collaborate with product managers and engineers to scope and prioritize feature requests based on merchant feedback and data, documenting requirements and success metrics for iterative releases.
Conduct competitive and market research on local commerce trends and merchant pain points to propose new growth initiatives aligned with DiDi’s regional strategies.
Monitor post-onboarding merchant health and retention signals, proposing targeted re-engagement tactics (promotions, training, co-marketing) to lift 30/60/90-day retention rates.



We're eager to be in touch because you have...


Currently pursuing a degree in Industrial Engineering, Business, Marketing, Economics, Statistics, or a related field is preferred but not mandatory.
Proficiency in data analysis using Excel
SQL, Python, or BI tools such as Tableau/Looke (nice to hacve)
Familiarity with digital marketing channels (SEM, social, in-app push, CRM) and experience running or supporting performance campaigns with measurable KPIs.
Strong communication and stakeholder management skills to work effectively with product, engineering, regional operations, and external merchant partners.
A results-oriented, analytical mindset with attention to detail, demonstrated by prior project work, internships, or coursework that includes measurable outcomes.
Ability to work in a fast-paced, cross-functional environment, prioritize multiple tasks, and adapt plans based on data and business needs.
Cultural sensitivity and local market awareness relevant to DiDi’s operating regions, with the ability to tailor merchant strategies to urban mobility and local commerce dynamics.



You'll love working at DiDi because...

We create user value

We strive to always create valuable experiences for our users in everything we do. Our focus is to always innovate new experiences that are safe, pleasant and efficient.

We are data-driven

We are strong believers in making informed decisions, that’s why we are data-driven. We can better navigate the business landscape strategically by analyzing valuable metrics.

Win-win Collaboration

Success is a team sport. When we work to help our partners and colleagues win, we win, too. While keeping everyone's best interest at heart, we communicate with candor and execute with excellence in all we do.

We believe in integrity

Integrity is at the very core of our business. We are people who always want to do the right thing. Our intentions are sincere, we speak our minds and listen to each other.

Growth

We always strive to do better. That means venturing beyond our comfort zones, learning from our mistakes, and helping each other grow.

Diversity and Inclusion

Diversity is one of our biggest strengths. Our differences are what make us distinct. We respect each other and believe in equal opportunities for all.

We are committed to building inclusive and diverse teams

At DiDi, we believe that our differences are our biggest source of strength. That’s why we are committed to promoting equal opportunities to all candidates and employees as an Equal Opportunity Employer.

Employment and advancement decisions at DiDi are always made based on the needs of the position and the qualifications of the candidate. We do not discriminate against any employee or applicant based on their gender, age, sexual orientation, nationality, marital status, pregnancy/maternity, disability, race, religion and beliefs, or any other status protected by applicable laws wherever we operate.

We are committed to building inclusive and diverse teams, and a workplace that is free from discrimination and harassment, because that’s how we create better products and services, make better decisions and better serve the communities we’re a part of.

At Roche you can show up as yourself, embraced for the unique qualities you bring. Our culture encourages personal expression, open dialogue, and genuine connections, where you are valued, accepted and respected for who you are, allowing you to thrive both personally and professionally. Un futuro más saludable. Es lo que nos impulsa a innovar. Who we are

A healthier future drives us to innovate. Let’s build a healthier future, together.

description:
About the job

Job Overview




Our client is looking for a Performance Marketing Specialist (Google Ads & Landing Pages) to execute and manage a high-volume digital marketing presence. In this role, you will build an initial batch of 20–100 landing pages for vehicle sales and parts/service campaigns using the Hercules AI platform, followed by creating 3–4 new landing pages daily for incoming used car inventory. This position requires a proactive professional who can seamlessly connect landing pages to Google Ads and Meta Ads for ongoing promotion while ensuring brand consistency and messaging alignment across all digital marketing assets.




Schedule




Monday - Friday, 10:00 AM - 3:00 PM MST/MDT (25 work hours per week)




Responsibilities




Build and maintain a high volume of landing pages for vehicle sales and parts/service campaigns using the Hercules AI platform.
Create and publish individual landing pages for new used car inventory on a daily basis to keep up with incoming stock.
Set up, manage, and optimize Google Ads and Meta Ads campaigns to drive targeted traffic to the built landing pages.
Connect all landing pages to paid advertising networks and coordinate media strategy across search and social channels to maximize ROI.
Monitor landing page conversion rates, interpret campaign data, and adjust active campaigns to improve ongoing performance.
Track and report on key digital marketing metrics across all active campaigns to maintain visibility on acquisition costs.
Ensure brand consistency, proper messaging alignment, and quality control across all digital marketing assets and public campaigns.




Requirements




Proven experience managing paid per click (PPC) campaigns on both Google Ads and Meta Ads platforms.
Strong understanding of landing page best practices, user experience, and conversion rate optimization (CRO).
Ability to manage multiple paid campaigns and high volumes of landing pages simultaneously with an exceptional level of organization.
Strong analytical skills with a demonstrated ability to interpret multi-channel campaign data and make quick, data-driven decisions.
Professional English written communication skills combined with a rigorous attention to detail for ad copy and landing page deployment.
Capability to execute technical platform tasks, such as tracking integrations, while reducing the client's direct hands-on involvement.




Independent Contractor Perks




Permanent work from home
Immediate hiring




Note




Please click the "Apply" button to complete your application, including the assessment questions, technical check, and voice recording. Your hourly pay rate will be established based on your performance in the application process; submissions with all requirements fulfilled will receive priority review.

description:
About the job

Acerca de nosotros

Somos una agencia global, especialista en medios, marketing y tecnología. Líderes en el ramo con presencia en más de 100 países. Creamos soluciones integrales para todos nuestros clientes de forma rápida y eficaz.

Nuestra búsqueda constante de mejorar en lo que hacemos comienza contactando con personas como tú.




OMG, te invita a unirte a nuestro equipo como: “Ejecutivo de medios (Offline Executive)"




¿Cuáles son las principales actividades?

1.- Generar información para la elaboración de la planeación táctica de la marca (bases de datos, competencia, tendencia de audiencia).

2.-Compra del plan aprobado por el cliente, respetando la duración y objetivos establecidos.

3.- Ingresar cada campaña al sistema de compras de la empresa para generar las órdenes de compra.

4.- Enviar a los proveedores las órdenes de compra generadas para respaldar las compras.

5.- Enviar a los proveedores los materiales creativos para poder iniciarla campaña.

Rebuild our existing relationships. We have lists of past and dormant influencers. A big part of this role is reigniting those relationships and turning old contacts back into active, converting partners.

Proactive: You see the gap and fill it. You bring the plan, you do not wait to be told.

description:
About the job

Affiliate & Influencer Marketing Specialist

TopFoxx | Remote | Full-Time

 

Location: Remote (must work U.S. East Coast hours).

Hours: Monday to Friday, 9:00 AM to 6:00 PM EST. Hours are flexible. We care about performance, not clocks.

Salary: $1,500 to $2,000 per month based on experience, plus performance bonuses tied to affiliate revenue and profitability.

Language: Perfect written and spoken English is required (this is non-negotiable), along with a deep understanding of U.S. culture, slang, and social media behavior.




About TopFoxx

TopFoxx is a fast-growing, women-owned fashion startup reinventing the sunglasses industry with solution-oriented designs and bold branding. Since 2016, we have grown from a bootstrapped e-commerce brand into a global business serving hundreds of thousands of customers, backed by an engaged community of influencers and creators.

We are not here to follow trends. We create them. Our sunglasses are tangle-free, floatable, foldable, shatter-resistant, and sustainably made, and we are just getting started.

We are a small, lean, founder-led team of fewer than ten people, with only about three of us on marketing. That means there is nowhere to hide and no one to pass the work to. Everyone wears multiple hats, owns real outcomes, and what you build genuinely moves the company. We do not do slow. We move fast, we pivot often, and we only hire A-players who treat the business like their own.




The Role

You will be the main one. This is the person who owns influencer and affiliate marketing end to end at TopFoxx. You are not waiting for a manual or a list of tasks. You are expected to bring the strategy, bring the ideas, and then execute them yourself, fast.

This space changes daily. New creators, new trends, new platform rules. You have to move quickly, test constantly, double down on what converts, and cut what does not. We are not looking for someone who only posts and reports. We are looking for a real executor who is proactive, creative, and obsessed with one thing above all: profitable, converting partnerships.

This is a big opportunity, and we want to be honest with you: it is also real work. We already have a large base of affiliates and creators, but your job is to grow it, activate it, and make it far more profitable. This is not a coast-along role. You will be building, pushing, and problem-solving every single day. For the right person, that is exactly the appeal.

This is a high-ownership role with a direct path into leading the influencer marketing function as it scales.




Why This Is Worth It

Here is the honest pitch. This is not a maintenance job and it is not busywork. Influencer and affiliate marketing is one of the biggest growth levers we have, and we believe this channel can become a multi-million dollar engine for TopFoxx. We are looking for the person who can build that.

We reward outcomes, not hours. If you can show us you can grow this and drive real, profitable revenue, you grow with it. That means a clear path into leadership and compensation that reflects the results you create. The ceiling here is high for someone who actually delivers, and you will be able to point to a business you helped build, not just tasks you completed.

If you want to be a small piece of a big machine, this is not it. If you want real ownership, real upside, and to look back in a year knowing you built something that matters, keep reading.




1. TikTok Shop & Affiliate Program (Your Primary Focus)

This is the heart of the role and where most of your time and energy goes. The big one is affiliate. Your job is to make sure the affiliates are converting, the program is profitable, and the numbers in TikTok Shop Seller Center are strong and trending up.

Own TikTok Shop Seller Center day to day. Live in the dashboards, know your numbers cold, and make sure performance is healthy across GMV, conversion, and orders.
Make the affiliate program profitable. Track and grow GMV, revenue per creator, sample-to-sales ROI, and commission costs. Protect margin. Every dollar of product and commission has to earn its return.
Build and manage a pipeline of hundreds to thousands of active TikTok Shop affiliates, and keep them actually posting and selling, not just signing up.
Recruit high-converting creators and live sellers who know how to talk about product and drive sales, not just collect free samples.
Go live and sell yourself. You will host TikTok Lives, get on camera, and sell the glasses directly. Then you bring affiliates on, show them how it is done, and coach them to do the same.
Coordinate TikTok Live schedules with strong sellers, secure Spark Ads permissions, and optimize listings and content angles for maximum GMV.
Watch performance weekly. Scale top converters aggressively, and cut low performers fast.
Keep the data honest. No padded numbers, no inflated reporting. Decisions are only as good as the data behind them.




2. Instagram Influencers

Instagram is the second pillar. Here we work on gifting and affiliate only, so your skill is finding the right people and getting them to convert without a paid budget.

Source Instagram influencers who can actually sell, creators who understand product and know how to drive their audience to buy, not just post a pretty photo.
Run gifting-only and affiliate-only collaborations, and move creators from one gift to repeat posting to consistent affiliate sales.
Rebuild our existing relationships. We have lists of past and dormant influencers. A big part of this role is reigniting those relationships and turning old contacts back into active, converting partners.
Build genuine, consistent relationships so TopFoxx stays top of mind, through thoughtful, regular touchpoints across Instagram and DMs.




3. LTK, Affiliate Platforms & Onboarding Calls

The third pillar is affiliate-driven platforms like LTK, and just as important, getting on calls with creators. This role is not behind a screen all day. You have to be outgoing and genuinely good at talking to people.

Recruit and activate creators on LTK and other affiliate networks (Amazon, affiliate links) who are still actively driving commission-based sales.
Run weekly onboarding calls. You will be on calls regularly, onboarding new influencers, walking them through the program, and getting them excited and set up to post.
Get on calls and close collabs. Be confident and persuasive, build rapport quickly, and convince creators to say yes to working with us, often with gifting only.
Understand creator psychology and motivate influencers to post consistently through relationship, community value, and a clear win for them.




4. Strategy, Reporting & Full Influencer Marketing Scope

Because you own this function, you also handle everything a strong e-commerce influencer marketing lead would handle. Study what bigger e-commerce brands do, then adapt the best of it to a lean, fast-moving team.

Build the strategy. Bring a clear point of view on where influencer and affiliate marketing is going and how TopFoxx wins, then put it into action.
Plan and execute campaigns tied to product launches, holidays, seasonal moments, and major sales events (BFCM, summer, new drops).
Manage content calendars, gifting coordination, sample sends, and follow-ups so nothing slips.
Build weekly performance reports that connect activity to revenue, and use them to decide what to scale and what to kill.
Create simple SOPs, trackers, and playbooks as you go, so the program is repeatable and scalable as the team grows.
Collaborate with the creative, marketing, and product teams to keep messaging aligned, and step in on urgent launch-related projects when needed.
Stay ahead of trends, new creators, and new affiliate opportunities before competitors do.
Help with wider marketing as needed. With a team this small, marketing is just three people and everyone pitches in. From time to time things will come up across other marketing areas and departments that need a hand. We need someone proactive and solution-oriented who brings energy, jumps in, and helps build this with us rather than sitting idle.




Who You Are

Proactive: You see the gap and fill it. You bring the plan, you do not wait to be told.
A real executor: You move fast, ship daily, and adapt as things change hour to hour.
Analytical and profit-obsessed: You can read a Seller Center dashboard, understand ROI and margin, and turn data into decisions.
Persuasive and outgoing: You love talking to creators, you are comfortable on camera and on calls, and you can sell the partnership and the product.
Relationship-driven: You are a natural connector who builds loyalty and keeps partners coming back.
Organized under pressure: You can juggle hundreds of moving pieces without dropping the ball.




Required Experience & Skills

2+ years in influencer or affiliate marketing, preferably in fashion, beauty, lifestyle, or e-commerce / DTC.
Expert, hands-on knowledge of TikTok Shop Seller Center mechanics and affiliate programs.
Proven track record of generating real sales through gifting-only or zero-budget strategies.
Comfort with LTK, Amazon, and other affiliate platforms.
Comfortable on camera and hosting TikTok Lives to sell product directly.
Perfect written and spoken English, and a strong understanding of U.S. culture and digital trends. This is genuinely important.
KPI-driven, highly organized, and able to build simple systems and SOPs as you scale.




What You Get

Salary of $1,500 to $2,000 per month based on experience, plus performance bonuses tied to affiliate revenue and profitability.
A fully remote role on a fast-moving global team.
Real ownership of the influencer and affiliate function, with a path into leadership as it scales.
PTO including vacation, holidays, sick, personal, and mental health days.
Free TopFoxx products and partner brand perks.

description:
About the job

I’m helping VenueAI find a top candidate to join their team full-time for the role of Customer Success & Onboarding Specialist — Sales Track.




You'll empower small businesses with AI, owning client success from onboarding to revenue growth.




Compensation:

USD 36K - 42K/year

+ Commissions (:USD5K/year)




Location:

Remote: Mexico




Mission of VenueAI:

"Our mission is to help venues and event spaces grow by using AI to make client communications smarter, faster, and more effective. We empower our customers to book more events, deliver better experiences, and focus on what they do best — creating unforgettable moments."




What makes you a strong candidate:

You are proficient in Troubleshooting, Software as a service (SaaS), Customer retention, Client onboarding, Account management.
You have the potential to develop in Marketing automation, Hospitality, Event management, CRM tools, Artificial intelligence (AI).
English - Fully fluent




Responsibilities and more:

VenueAI is a fast-growing SaaS company helping wedding and event venues book more events using AI. Our clients are relationship-driven small business owners, and the person in this role becomes one of their most trusted partners.




We're looking for someone who combines exceptional communication skills with strong technical aptitude. You'll own the onboarding experience end-to-end: leading client calls, managing the setup process yourself, and ensuring every new customer launches successfully and starts seeing results quickly. Beyond onboarding, you'll continue supporting clients through adoption, retention, renewals, and future growth opportunities, helping them feel confident and successful with VenueAI.




You won't be expected to be an engineer, and our technical team is available for more complex issues. However, you should be comfortable handling the day-to-day technical aspects of onboarding independently. As you deepen your product knowledge and strengthen client relationships, you'll have a clear path to expand into product demos, identifying expansion opportunities, and playing a larger role in revenue growth over time.




===WHAT YOU'LL DO===

* Serve as the warm, confident face of VenueAI during onboarding and client interactions




* Own the onboarding process end-to-end, including leading calls and managing technical setup such as integrations, forms, DNS configuration, and troubleshooting




* Ensure clients go live quickly and successfully by conducting quality assurance checks before launch




* Guide customers through the early adoption phase so they understand the product, realize value quickly, and feel confident using VenueAI




* Build strong client relationships that drive retention, renewals, and long-term success




* Identify opportunities for upgrades, expanded usage, and other growth initiatives as clients achieve success




* Develop deep product expertise to confidently address client questions and provide strategic guidance




* Partner with the technical team on complex issues while independently managing routine setup and support needs




* Grow into leading product demos and contributing to revenue-generating activities over time




* Identify friction points in onboarding and the customer journey, and help improve processes as we scale




===WHO YOU ARE===

* Naturally engaging and relationship-oriented; people trust and connect with you quickly




* Fluent or near-native in English, with strong verbal communication skills and confidence in client-facing video calls




* Technically capable and comfortable managing onboarding tasks such as integrations, forms, DNS setup, and basic troubleshooting without relying heavily on developers




* Consultative in your approach, focused on listening, building rapport, and guiding clients toward success




* Organized, proactive, and able to work independently in a fast-paced environment




* Experienced, with 2+ years in SaaS Customer Success, Implementation, Onboarding, or Account Management, ideally supporting US-based clients




* Aware that exceptional onboarding drives adoption, retention, renewals, and future revenue opportunities




* Excited by a role that combines customer success with a long-term path toward demos and sales responsibilities




===NICE TO HAVE===

* Experience transitioning from Customer Success or Onboarding into a demo or sales-focused role




* Familiarity with CRM platforms, marketing automation tools, or AI products




* Experience in the events, hospitality, or wedding industries




===THIS ROLE IS NOT FOR YOU IF===

* You prefer working exclusively behind the scenes and avoid client-facing interactions




* You don't enjoy building relationships and engaging directly with customers




* You depend on developers to resolve routine setup or integration tasks




* You're uncomfortable troubleshooting issues and finding solutions independently




* You want to remain strictly in a support role with no interest in demos, sales, or revenue-related responsibilities




===COMPENSATION & GROWTH===

* Base salary: USD $36,000–$42,000/year, depending on experience




* Performance bonus tied to onboarding success, retention, and client outcomes




* 100% remote, exclusive full-time role




* A clear growth path from onboarding → customer success → demos → sales as VenueAI continues to scale




Your potential leader(s):

Joaquin Corrales - Founder at VenueAI | Building the AI operating system for wedding and event venues
Ericka - Chief Operating Officer at VenueAI

description:
About the job

About GrowthX AI

At GrowthX, we’re building the modern growth engine for marketing teams. Since launching in 2024, we've grown from concept to eight-figure annual revenue. To date, we've raised a $12M Series A led by Madrona Venture Group and we partner with 60+ companies ranging from high-growth startups to industry leaders like Lovable, Webflow, Ramp, Superhuman, Strapi, Abnormal Security, and Augment Code.

We’ve built something different: an operating system that unifies powerful AI workflows with forward-deployed experts to transform content into compounding organic growth. We’re building the growth infrastructure marketing teams wish they’d always had — the system that reliably turns context into strategy, strategy into execution, and execution into measurable growth.

About The Role

We’re hiring an SEO Customer Success to own the most critical phase of the customer lifecycle: turning new customers into confident, long-term partners who see real growth and choose to expand with us.

This role is customer-facing, outcome-driven, and consultative. You are accountable for onboarding customers, leading early strategy and execution, and converting successful pilots or initial engagements into full-time contracts.

You are not just managing delivery — you are owning momentum.

You’ll work hands-on with customers to define growth strategies rooted in SEO, content, and AI visibility, translate those strategies into execution with our editors and specialists, and ensure customers clearly understand what’s working, why it’s working, and what to do next.

What You’ll Do


Own customer onboarding through early delivery and conversion to long-term contracts.
Lead customer strategy across SEO, content, and AI visibility with a clear focus on outcomes.
Run weekly or bi-weekly customer meetings and manage all day-to-day communication.
Translate performance metrics (traffic, rankings, AI visibility, conversions) into clear business impact.
Partner with editors and specialists to ensure high-quality, on-time execution.
Remove blockers quickly and step in directly when execution needs hands-on leadership.
Identify renewal and expansion opportunities and advance those conversations in partnership with Account Directors.
Share learnings and patterns back with the team to improve playbooks and delivery at scale.
Operate effectively in a fast-paced, high-ambiguity environment where priorities evolve and speed matters.


Who You Are


Deeply accountable for customer outcomes and momentum.
Consultative, confident, and credible with senior marketing leaders.
Hands-on — able to do the work, not just manage it.
Commercially minded and comfortable advancing conversion conversations.
Highly organized, responsive, and proactive.
Comfortable working autonomously in a distributed team, with strong judgment about when to escalate or collaborate.
Curious and experienced with AI tools, automation, and modern content systems.
Energized by fast-moving environments and building while doing.

4–7 years of experience in customer success, engagement management, account strategy, consulting, or agency delivery.

Proven experience owning customer relationships and driving renewals or expansions.

description:
About the job

This position is 100% remote for Mexico, Columbia, Venezuela and Guatemala, Argentina, Honduras, DR, Brazil applicants. Not a US based post. The hours are 10am-7pm MST and includes one Saturday a month from 8-5 MST.

About Us

The Savings Group (TSG), the parent company of RateGenius, AUTOPAY and Tresl, is the most diversified consumer marketplace for automotive finance, refinance, and protection plans. Through a network of more than 180 lenders across all 50 states, TSG provides consumers more choices for their origination and refinanced auto loans, while also delivering loan volume growth to its lender network and partners through a proprietary, web-based platform.

Why Work at The Savings Group?

Working at TSG is more than your standard J-O-B. That's because we work with some of the most amazing goal-oriented and driven people in fintech and still embody the spirit of a startup! Not only have we worked together to perfect the way we serve our customers, but we've built a pretty awesome culture in the process. Our people come from all kinds of backgrounds to create something exciting and new: A BETTER WAY TO DO CAR LOANS. We offer steady growth, a strong and supportive leadership team, and in-depth training and tools to ensure your success.

Summary

The Customer Success Representative Level I ensures that customers receive wonderful customer service in all aspects when taking care of the customers. This includes any and all customers that are calling in to inquire regarding potential vehicle purchases, refinances or questions regarding open applications and so forth. Customer Success maintains all overflow through TSG phone systems and is the start of the TSG journey for all our customers throughout all of its brands. Customer Success Representatives Level I are required to answer all phone calls received, respond to customer inquiries, complete customer applications either over the phone or via text/email, and send emails to the correct corresponding departments as needed to resolve customer complaints. This includes internal transfers as well as maintaining Legal and Compliance concerns when completing applications as well. 

ESSENTIAL DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES include achieving positive results in the following key areas:


Understand and demonstrate principles of The Savings Group’s Mission, Vision, and Values. 
Document all account, contact, and activity information into various systems. 
Answer calls from internal and external sources, while providing a world-class customer service experience. 
Complete applications by utilizing various systems to obtain the necessary information and consent
Manage inbound and outbound communication by email, phone and/ or text to customers to complete applications
Ensure to send communications to the correct departments when the customer calls in for something that goes beyond the scope of what you are able to assist with (i.e, titles issues, escalations, payoff issues, loan specialist request etc..)
Consistently respond to auto finance inquiries in a timely, efficient, and professional manner. 
Meet all metrics and standards set forth by management
Additional duties as required. 


QUALIFICATIONS

To perform this job successfully, an individual must be able to perform each essential duty satisfactorily. The requirements listed below are representative of the knowledge, skill, and/or ability required. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions.

Knowledge, Skills, And Abilities


Education: High School diploma is required. One (1) year of customer service-related experience in a call center preferred
Experience: Experience in inside phone sales, telemarketing, quality assurance or customer service experience with auto finance companies or, car dealerships
Language/Communication/Interpersonal: Strong listening, verbal, and written communication skills. Ability to read and comprehend simple instructions, short correspondence, and memos. Ability to write simple correspondence. Ability to effectively present information to customers, clients, and all levels of management
Reasoning Ability: Ability to apply common sense understanding to carry out detailed but uninvolved written or oral instructions. Ability to deal with problems involving concrete variables in standardized situations
Analytical: Detail-oriented with ability to organize, prioritize and work independently. Strong critical thinking skills
Technical: Ability to toggle between applications on a dual monitor setup. Proficiency with Microsoft Office, with intermediate Excel ability, required. Keyboarding/computer skills, heavy computer and phone usage
Other: High level of personal motivation, professionalism and integrity required. Ability to exercise discretion and judgment. Must be dependable


Computer IT/Requirements/compensation:

Must have a desktop or laptop PC capable of installing and running the Windows App. Device must support hardwired (Ethernet) connection for stable network access.


Reliable internet connection with minimum speeds of:
Download: 50 Mbps or higher. Upload: 10 Mbps or higher
Must provide their own headset, mouse, and keyboard

$1500 US Dollars per month paid on the last day of each month.

description:
About the job

Job Title: Senior Customer Success Specialist – Budgeting & Reporting
Work Hours: Part-Time (20 hours per week), transition into a Full-Time (40 hours per week) position within 3 to 6 months
Schedule/Timezone: Days of the week to be determined, 9:00 AM – 1:00 PM (with flexibility) / PST
Location: 100% Remote (Latin America Only)




Overview




Are you a finance or accounting professional who loves solving software puzzles and helping clients succeed?




We are a leading financial budgeting, forecasting, and reporting software provider designed specifically to empower mid-market corporate finance teams. Operating at the intersection of FinTech and Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP), we specialize in creating seamless software solutions that integrate with major accounting ecosystems like Microsoft Dynamics, Sage Intacct, Oracle NetSuite, and Acumatica. Our mission is to eliminate the headache of complex financial planning for CFOs, Controllers, and Finance Managers across diverse industries, including healthcare, insurance, and nonprofits.




In this role, you won't be reading from a scripted call-center prompt. Instead, you will act as a true consultative partner, leveraging your financial acumen to guide users through intricate software workflows, troubleshoot complex reporting queries, and help organizations manage massive operations smoothly.




Key Responsibilities




Conduct live, consultative Zoom and Microsoft Teams support and training sessions with corporate finance users
Guide customers through critical financial software workflows, including budget roll forwards, forecast creation, payroll forecasting, allocations, and ERP data exports/imports
Efficiently respond to incoming customer emails and technical support tickets regarding workflow and reporting concerns
Support customer onboarding initiatives, shadow senior team members on demo calls, and help clients implement operational best practices
Update customer-facing Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs), author clear help topics and FAQ documentation, and log recurring issues to optimize internal support processes




Must-Have Requirements




Strong, bilingual spoken and written communication skills in both English and Spanish
Minimum of 3 years of hands-on experience working directly with financial budgeting or reporting software platforms
A polished, professional customer presence with the confidence to lead live screenshare troubleshooting sessions and ask clarifying questions
Deep understanding of the operational challenges involved in managing large, complex corporate budgets
Direct experience creating SOPs, training materials, or technical knowledge base articles
Highly proficient with Microsoft Excel, standard ticketing/helpdesk systems, and virtual meeting tools (Zoom/Teams)
A reliable high-speed internet connection, a quiet dedicated home office environment, a functional webcam, and a dual-monitor setup




Nice-to-Have Requirements




Prior support experience specifically tailored to ERP systems or mid-market accounting software
Hands-on experience utilizing industry-standard helpdesk software such as Zendesk, Freshdesk, or Zoho Desk
Familiarity or exposure to specialized financial reporting tools, business intelligence platforms, or budgeting software (e.g., Power BI, SQL, Solver, Jet Reports, Velixo, Vena, Prophix, Planful, Adaptive Planning, Datarails, Martus, or Forecaster)




Why Join Us




This is an exceptional entry point into an innovative tech space that values your accounting and financial expertise. We believe in setting our team up for long-term success, which is why you will receive comprehensive, hands-on training—from shadowing live support calls to practicing in dedicated demo environments before taking full ownership of client accounts.

description:
About the job

Our Client is one of the United States' largest insurers, providing a wide range of insurance and financial services products with gross written premiums well over US$25 Billion (P&C). They proudly serve more than 10 million U.S. households with more than 19 million individual policies across all 50 states through the efforts of over 48,000 exclusive and independent agents and nearly 18,500 employees. Finally, our Client is part of one the largest Insurance Groups in the world.

Senior Operations Analyst (Call Center)

Location: [Insert Location]

Company: Farmers

About The Role

We are seeking a highly analytical and operations-focused Senior Operations Analyst to support the day-to-day management of contact center performance. This role is responsible for overseeing real-time operations, monitoring call volume and agent adherence, and ensuring resources are allocated effectively to meet service level objectives.

The ideal candidate has a strong background in contact center operations, workforce management, and operational analytics. You will work closely with leadership and cross-functional teams to identify trends, manage operational risks, optimize performance, and recommend solutions that improve efficiency, customer experience, and business outcomes.

This position requires strong analytical skills, the ability to make data-driven decisions in a fast-paced environment, and experience balancing operational priorities while maintaining service levels.

What You'll Do


 Monitor and manage real-time contact center performance, including call volume, queue management, service levels, and agent adherence 
 Oversee daily operational activities to ensure efficient allocation of resources and achievement of performance targets 
 Analyze operational data to identify trends, risks, and opportunities for improvement 
 Partner with workforce management, operations leadership, and business stakeholders to support staffing and service delivery decisions 
 Coordinate and support operational projects, initiatives, and process improvements across the contact center environment 
 Conduct audits and reviews of operational processes, procedures, and business initiatives to evaluate effectiveness and efficiency 
 Develop reports, dashboards, and performance analyses to support operational decision-making 
 Investigate operational issues and recommend solutions to improve productivity, customer experience, and business performance 
 Assess the impact of business changes, new initiatives, and external factors on operational performance 
 Communicate operational insights, recommendations, and risks to leadership and key stakeholders 
 Support continuous improvement efforts by identifying process optimization opportunities and implementing best practices 
 Collaborate with cross-functional teams to ensure consistency and alignment across operational functions


Requirements


 Experience in contact center operations, workforce management, operational analytics, or a related field 
 Overall business analysis, operations analysis, or data analysis experience preferred 
 Experience managing real-time operations, call queues, service levels, and workforce adherence in a contact center environment 
 Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with experience interpreting operational data and performance metrics 
 Experience with NICE IEX Workforce Management (WFM) systems 
 Familiarity with Cisco ACD and contact center telephony platforms 
 Strong Microsoft Office skills, particularly Excel for data analysis and reporting 
 Experience with data mining, operational reporting, and performance analysis 
 Ability to influence stakeholders and communicate recommendations effectively to leadership teams 
 Strong project coordination and organizational skills with the ability to manage multiple priorities simultaneously


Benefits


Competitive salary and performance-based bonuses
Comprehensive benefits package
Career development and training opportunities
Flexible work arrangements
Dynamic and inclusive work culture within a globally renowned group

title: Order Fulfillment Specialist – Customer Success
skills: Receive, review, and validate customer purchase orders to ensure accuracy of pricing, contractual terms, delivery requirements, and compliance with internal policies.
Request and coordinate product allocation, confirm material availability, and enter customer orders accurately into internal systems while monitoring order status through fulfillment.

Proactively track and resolve order delays or discrepancies, collaborating cross-functionally with Sales, Supply Chain, Planning, Finance and Customer Success teams to align on delivery timelines and customer expectations.
Generate and distribute sales order confirmations to customers, ensuring clear communication of order details and fulfillment milestones.
Document, verify, and maintain records of any applicable third-party broker commissions associated with customer orders, ensuring accuracy and audit readiness.
Plan, select, and execute all outbound customer shipments, balancing, cost efficiency, service reliability, and customer-specific delivery requirements.
Monitor shipment progress from dispatch through final delivery, providing timely, proactive updates to customers and internal stakeholders.
Coordinate closely with freight brokers, carriers, and warehouse partners to schedule shipments, resolve transit issues, mitigate delays, develop contingency plans to minimize customer impact.
Identify recurring logistics challenges and contribute to continuous improvement efforts aimed at enhancing delivery performance, visibility, and overall customer satisfaction.
Build and maintain strong customer relationships through proactive communication, responsiveness, and a solutions-oriented approach that reinforces thrust and long-term partnership.
Act as an extension of the Sales team by supporting customer requests, identifying upsell or follow-on order opportunities, and ensuring seamless handoffs between Sales and Operations.

Communicate extensively with customers, carriers, brokers, and internal teams via high-volume email and time-sensitive phone calls, responding promptly to inquiries, issues, and escalation needs related to active orders and shipments.

Prepare, retrieve, and distribute customer-requested documentation such as invoices, packing lists, certificates, and shipping records, ensuring accuracy and compliance with customer and regulatory requirements.
Carry out other duties to support departmental and organizational objectives.
description:
About the job

Principal Function: The Order Fulfillment Specialist - Customer Success plays a critical role in efficiently receiving, reviewing, and processing all inbound customer purchase orders. This position ensures seamless coordination between customers, sales and internal teams, facilitates accurate order entry and timely fulfillment, and contributes to maintaining organized, compliant, and accurate internal processes.




Essential Duties and Responsibilities: Duties include, but are not limited to:




1. Customer Order Processing

Receive, review, and validate customer purchase orders to ensure accuracy of pricing, contractual terms, delivery requirements, and compliance with internal policies.
Request and coordinate product allocation, confirm material availability, and enter customer orders accurately into internal systems while monitoring order status through fulfillment.
Proactively track and resolve order delays or discrepancies, collaborating cross-functionally with Sales, Supply Chain, Planning, Finance and Customer Success teams to align on delivery timelines and customer expectations.
Generate and distribute sales order confirmations to customers, ensuring clear communication of order details and fulfillment milestones.
Document, verify, and maintain records of any applicable third-party broker commissions associated with customer orders, ensuring accuracy and audit readiness.

2. Customer Outbound Logistics

Plan, select, and execute all outbound customer shipments, balancing, cost efficiency, service reliability, and customer-specific delivery requirements.
Monitor shipment progress from dispatch through final delivery, providing timely, proactive updates to customers and internal stakeholders.
Coordinate closely with freight brokers, carriers, and warehouse partners to schedule shipments, resolve transit issues, mitigate delays, develop contingency plans to minimize customer impact.
Identify recurring logistics challenges and contribute to continuous improvement efforts aimed at enhancing delivery performance, visibility, and overall customer satisfaction.

3. Customer Support

Build and maintain strong customer relationships through proactive communication, responsiveness, and a solutions-oriented approach that reinforces thrust and long-term partnership.
Act as an extension of the Sales team by supporting customer requests, identifying upsell or follow-on order opportunities, and ensuring seamless handoffs between Sales and Operations.
Communicate extensively with customers, carriers, brokers, and internal teams via high-volume email and time-sensitive phone calls, responding promptly to inquiries, issues, and escalation needs related to active orders and shipments.
Prepare, retrieve, and distribute customer-requested documentation such as invoices, packing lists, certificates, and shipping records, ensuring accuracy and compliance with customer and regulatory requirements.

4. Other

Carry out other duties to support departmental and organizational objectives.







Supervisory Responsibilities: This job has no supervisory responsibilities.

Qualifications: To perform this job successfully, an individual must be able to perform each essential duty according to the requirements of the Company. The requirements listed below are representative of the knowledge, skills, and/or abilities required. Reasonable accommodation may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential duties and responsibilities.




Company-Related Competencies: To perform their jobs successfully, all employees working in the St. Louis Group should demonstrate the following competencies:

· Ethics – Treats people with respect; Keeps commitments; Inspires the trust of others; Works ethically and with integrity; Upholds Company values.

· Professionalism – Approaches others in a tactful manner; Reacts well under pressure; Treats others with respect and consideration regardless of their status or position; Accepts responsibility for personal actions; Follows through on commitments.

· Problem Solving – Identifies and resolves problems in a timely manner; Gathers and analyzes information skillfully; Develops alternative solutions; Works well in group problem solving situations; Uses reason even when dealing with emotional topics.

· Written and Oral Communication – Speaks clearly and persuasively in positive or negative situations; Listens and obtains clarification; Responds well to questions; Demonstrates group presentation skills; Participates in meetings; Writes clearly and informatively; Edits work for spelling and grammar; Varies writing style to meet needs; Presents numerical data effectively; Reads with the ability to interpret written information.

· Teamwork – Balances team and individual responsibilities; Exhibits objectivity and openness to others’ views; Gives and welcomes feedback; Contributes to building a positive team spirit; Puts success of team above own interests; Able to build morale and group commitments to goals and objectives; Supports everyone’s efforts to succeed.

· Adaptability – Adapts to changes in the work environment; Manages competing demands; Changes approach or method to best fit the situation; Able to deal with frequent change, delays or unexpected events.




Job-Related Competencies – To perform this job successfully, employees in this job should demonstrate the following competencies:

· Quality and Quantity – Demonstrates accuracy and thoroughness; Looks for ways to improve and promote quality; Applies feedback to improve performance; Monitors own work to ensure quality; Meets productivity standards; Completes work in timely manner; Strives to increase productivity; Works quickly but efficiently.

· Customer Service – Manages difficult or emotional customer situations; Responds promptly to customer needs; Solicits customer feedback to improve service; Responds to requests for service and assistance; Meets commitments.

· Dependability – Follows instructions; Responds to management direction; Takes responsibility for own actions; Keeps commitments; Commits to long hours of work when necessary to reach goals; Completes tasks on time or notifies appropriate person with an alternate plan.

· Organizational Support – Follows policies and procedures; Completes administrative tasks correctly and on time; Supports organization’s goals and values; Benefits organization through outside activities; Supports affirmative action and respects diversity.

· Attendance/Punctuality – Is consistently at work and is on time; Ensures work responsibilities are covered when absent; Arrives at meetings and appointments on time.




Education and/or Experience: Bachelor's Degree and two-years related experience and/or training in an corporate office setting; or equivalent combination of education and experience.




Other Skills and Abilities: The following skills and abilities are either required or desired.

· Computer software skills including: Microsoft Office Suite (Outlook, Word, Excel), Adobe, and Slack messaging software are required. The ability to quickly adapt and embrace new software is required.

· The ability to develop positive working relationships and interact effectively with vendors, brokers, customers, and colleagues is required.

· The ability to effectively listen and communicate orally and in writing is required.

· The ability to manage and prioritize multiple projects simultaneously is required.

· The ability to prepare reports and forms is required.

· The ability to follow all established protocols, guidelines, and departmental policies is required.

· The ability to work alone with minimum supervision and with others in a team environment is required.

· The ability to work rapidly for long periods, under time pressure, and on several tasks at the same time is required.

· The ability to remain calm, perform duties, and make sound and rational decisions, including exercising independent judgment within established protocols and guidelines is required.

· The ability to be sensitive to professional ethics, gender, cultural diversities and disabilities is required.




· A working knowledge of standard office practices and the ability to apply such knowledge to a variety of interrelated processes, tasks, and operations is required.

· The ability to operate a computer, printer, telephone/switchboard, calculator, postage meter, copy machine, scanner, fax machine, shredder, and other office equipment is required.

· The ability to speak, read and write in English is required. Spanish is desired.

· The ability to occasionally travel out-of-town on Company-related business and/or to attend conferences and trainings, is desired.




Work Environment: The work environment characteristics described here are representative of those an employee encounters while performing the essential functions of this job. Reasonable accommodation may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential duties and responsibilities. While performing the duties of this job, the employee may be exposed to the risk of electrical shock when working with the St. Louis Group office equipment. The noise level in the work environment is usually quiet to moderate. While the offices of the St. Louis Group are usually open from 9:00a.m. to 5:00 p.m., Monday through Friday, this position may require work beyond these hours and on the weekends

description:
About the job

About The Company

DiDi Global Inc. is the world’s leading mobility technology platform. It offers a wide range of app-based services across markets including Asia-Pacific, Latin America and Africa, including ride hailing, taxi hailing, chauffeur, hitch and other forms of shared mobility as well as auto solutions, food delivery, intra-city freight, and financial services.

DiDi provides car owners, drivers, and delivery partners with flexible work and income opportunities. It is committed to collaborating with policymakers, the taxi industry, the automobile industry and the communities to solve the world’s transportation, environmental and employment challenges through the use of AI technology and localized smart transportation innovations. DiDi strives to create better life experiences and greater social value, by building a safe, inclusive and sustainable transportation and local services ecosystem for cities of the future.

For more information, please visit: www.didiglobal.com/news

About The Team/role

In this role, you’ll be at the forefront of designing and executing high-impact advertising strategies, working cross-functionally (Product, Marketing, Operations) and partnering with top-tier clients.

We’re looking for a strategic, data-driven, and resourceful trailblazer who thrives on challenges and is eager to elevate our Ads platform to the next level.

In this role, you'll be...


Developing and implementing innovative advertising strategies, from ideation to campaign execution, ensuring measurable business impact.
Collaborating with internal teams (Product, Marketing, Data, Operations) to optimize ad performance and deliver an exceptional customer experience.
Building and strengthening relationships with external partners (global brands, agencies, new advertisers), negotiating deals that drive the adoption of our advertising solutions.
Analyzing performance metrics and leveraging data insights to identify continuous improvements and maximize campaign outcomes.
Influencing product development by proposing new ad formats and contributing to the long-term vision of DiDi Ads.
Supporting and guiding your team and stakeholders, fostering a culture of win-win collaboration, integrity, and ongoing growth.
Delivering impactful presentations to executives and clients, showcasing the value and benefits of our advertising solutions



We're eager to be in touch because you have...


5 years of experience in digital advertising strategy, ideally within large-scale platforms or fast-paced environments.
A proven track record in negotiation, account management, and consultative sales for enterprise-level clients, backed by strong communication skills.
A bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Business, Engineering, or a related field.
Advanced data analysis capabilities to make insight-driven decisions.
An agile and collaborative mindset, aligned with our values of creating value, data-based thinking, win-win collaboration, integrity, growth, and diversity.
Professional fluency in English, allowing you to confidently present ideas and influence global teams.



You'll love working at DiDi because...

We create user value

We strive to always create valuable experiences for our users in everything we do. Our focus is to always innovate new experiences that are safe, pleasant and efficient.

We are data-driven

We are strong believers in making informed decisions, that’s why we are data-driven. We can better navigate the business landscape strategically by analyzing valuable metrics.

Win-win Collaboration

Success is a team sport. When we work to help our partners and colleagues win, we win, too. While keeping everyone's best interest at heart, we communicate with candor and execute with excellence in all we do.

We believe in integrity

Integrity is at the very core of our business. We are people who always want to do the right thing. Our intentions are sincere, we speak our minds and listen to each other.

Growth

We always strive to do better. That means venturing beyond our comfort zones, learning from our mistakes, and helping each other grow.

Diversity and Inclusion

Diversity is one of our biggest strengths. Our differences are what make us distinct. We respect each other and believe in equal opportunities for all.

We are committed to building inclusive and diverse teams

At DiDi, we believe that our differences are our biggest source of strength. That’s why we are committed to promoting equal opportunities to all candidates and employees as an Equal Opportunity Employer.

Employment and advancement decisions at DiDi are always made based on the needs of the position and the qualifications of the candidate. We do not discriminate against any employee or applicant based on their gender, age, sexual orientation, nationality, marital status, pregnancy/maternity, disability, race, religion and beliefs, or any other status protected by applicable laws wherever we operate.

We are committed to building inclusive and diverse teams, and a workplace that is free from discrimination and harassment, because that’s how we create better products and services, make better decisions and better serve the communities we’re a part of.

Job Summary:

As a Marketing and Customer Analytics Consultant, you will lead the design, implementation, and optimization of analytics solutions for clients. Your role will involve conducting user experience assessments, managing data processes, and delivering actionable insights to improve marketing initiatives and customer journeys.

Roles & Responsibilities:



Conduct user experience assessments on websites/apps and provide recommendations to optimize analytics stacks.

Gather business requirements and translate complex functional, technical, and business needs into architectural designs and solution blueprints.

Manage processes for capturing, storing, using, and tracking online activity data from multiple channels.

Use consultative skills, business knowledge, and technical expertise to integrate analytics technologies into clients’ digital platforms and achieve business results.

Analyze integration aspects between web analytics and allied systems like websites, apps, campaign managers, audience managers, Firebase, and tag managers.

Plan and design digital analytics/clickstream data inclusion in customer 360 views and provide frameworks, solutions, architectures, and roadmaps for clients.

Understand customer journeys and current trends to provide recommendations for improving marketing initiatives, personalization, and advertising strategies.

Identify, scope, and partner with technology solution providers and marketing agencies to deliver tailored solutions.

Work with technology teams to orchestrate and implement integrations, including API-based integrations.

Understand and convey the impact of regulations like HIPAA, CCPA, and GDPR on marketing channels.

Mobilize and organize across multiple stakeholders and projects with strong project management skills.

Drive business development efforts and thought leadership in the digital marketing platform space.



Professional & Technical Skills:



Must Have Skills: website/app and marketing analytics tools like Adobe Analytics (Priority), Adobe Target, Google Analytics, Adobe CJA, NetInsight, Appsflyer, WebTrends, Optimizely, SEMRush, and Firebase.

Hands-on experience in delivering and executing technical/functional design solutions.

Good to Have Skills: Experience with tag management platforms (GTM, Adobe Launch, Tealium), Ads conversion pixels, DMPs, and visualization tools like Tableau, PowerBI, and Google Data Studio.



Your experience counts!



Bachelor’s degree completed; MBA is highly desirable.

Advanced level of English and excellent oral and written communication skills 

+5 years of proven working experience in developing and executing data-driven marketing, web, and customer analytics across industries such as E-Commerce, CPG, Telecom, Healthcare, and Financial Services.

Experience in projects with Adobe Platforms.

Strong understanding of collaboration between web/app analytics and other marketing, advertising, and personalization technologies.

Preferred but not mandatory: Experience working with Ads Data Hub, ad platforms, and their APIs (e.g., Google API, Facebook Conversion API).

Hands-on functional consulting experience preferably in Adobe marketing processes, campaign lifecycles, and integration with other MarTech platforms implementation with deep functional, industry and client facing experience

description:
About the job

🚀 ¿Te apasiona el Performance Marketing y te motiva convertir inversión en crecimiento real?

Buscamos una persona que quiera liderar la estrategia de adquisición digital de una compañía tecnológica con presencia internacional, convirtiendo los datos en decisiones y las campañas en oportunidades reales de negocio.

Formarás parte de un entorno donde Marketing tiene un impacto directo en el crecimiento de la empresa. Tendrás autonomía para definir estrategias, optimizar inversiones y experimentar continuamente con nuevos canales, trabajando muy cerca de los equipos de Marketing, Ventas y Revenue.

Si disfrutas analizando métricas, optimizando campañas y escalando resultados mediante decisiones basadas en datos, este reto puede ser para ti.

🎯 Tu misión

Serás responsable de liderar la estrategia de Performance Marketing para Latinoamérica, impulsando la captación digital desde México y adaptando las campañas a las necesidades de cada mercado de la región.

Tendrás ownership sobre todo el ciclo de adquisición: desde la planificación de campañas y la gestión del presupuesto publicitario hasta la optimización continua del rendimiento y el análisis de resultados. Trabajarás de forma muy cercana con los equipos de Marketing, Ventas y Revenue para garantizar que cada euro invertido contribuya al crecimiento del negocio y a la generación de oportunidades de calidad.

🚀 ¿Qué harás en tu día a día?

Elaborar informes periódicos con insights y recomendaciones que permitan mejorar el rendimiento de las campañas y apoyar la toma de decisiones.

👀 Buscamos a alguien que...

✔ Cuente con entre 3 y 5 años de experiencia en Performance Marketing, Paid Media o Growth Marketing.

✔ Haya gestionado campañas de adquisición de forma autónoma en Google Ads y Meta Ads.

✔ Tenga experiencia optimizando campañas orientadas a generación de leads, adquisición de clientes y crecimiento de ingresos.

✔ Se sienta cómodo/a gestionando presupuestos publicitarios y tomando decisiones basadas en métricas de negocio.

✔ Tenga experiencia trabajando con herramientas de reporting y visualización de datos como Looker Studio, Power BI o similares.

✔ Esté familiarizado/a con CRM como HubSpot, Salesforce, Microsoft Dynamics o equivalentes para el seguimiento del ciclo de vida del lead.

✔ Posea una fuerte capacidad analítica y disfrute convirtiendo datos en oportunidades de mejora.

⭐ Valoraremos especialmente si además...

description:
About the job

Great companies don’t grow because they post more content. They grow because the right people notice them.




Every day, potential clients are scrolling, searching, and deciding who they trust.




The challenge isn't getting attention—it's turning attention into conversations, opportunities, and growth.




That's why we're looking for a Marketing & Growth Coordinator who can help us expand our reach, strengthen our brand presence, and create meaningful connections with future clients.




This is a hands-on, execution-focused role that combines content creation, audience growth, outbound marketing, prospect engagement, and first-touch client communication. You'll play a direct role in helping us generate inbound and outbound opportunities while supporting the growth of our business pipeline.




If you're someone who enjoys turning ideas into action, creating engaging content, building relationships, and helping businesses grow, we'd love to meet you.




What You'll Do

Drive Marketing & Growth Initiatives

Develop and execute marketing initiatives designed to attract new clients and increase company visibility.
Support pipeline growth through outbound campaigns, targeted outreach, and brand awareness efforts.
Identify and engage industries, markets, and niches aligned with company growth goals.
Help generate inbound business opportunities through consistent and strategic marketing efforts.
Contribute directly to company growth by creating opportunities that lead to new client conversations.




Build and Grow Our Social Presence

Manage and maintain company social media platforms, including LinkedIn, Instagram, Facebook, and other relevant channels.
Create and publish high-quality content that showcases company activity, hiring successes, operational updates, and client outcomes.
Develop a consistent flow of written, visual, and video-based content.
Monitor engagement, interact with audiences when needed, and maintain an active online presence.
Help strengthen brand awareness and position the company as a trusted partner in the marketplace.




Execute Outbound & Inbound Marketing Efforts

Launch and manage outbound email and messaging campaigns tied to hiring activity, target industries, and company initiatives.
Build, organize, and maintain prospect outreach lists.
Handle inbound marketing inquiries and serve as the first point of contact for prospective clients.
Conduct first-touch discovery calls with potential clients.
Communicate professionally and confidently with U.S.-based business owners and decision-makers.




Create Content That Supports Growth

Develop marketing materials, including presentations, one-pagers, branded collateral, and other business development assets.
Coordinate simple video editing and content formatting.
Support company branding initiatives and ensure consistency across all platforms and materials.
Assist with branded materials, promotional items, and company marketing assets when needed.
Help tell the company's story through content that attracts, engages, and converts prospects.




Collaborate Across the Organization

Work closely with leadership and internal teams to align marketing efforts with company goals and priorities.
Gather updates, success stories, wins, and content from internal departments.
Support company-wide growth initiatives through consistent execution and follow-through.
Help ensure marketing efforts remain aligned with business objectives and growth targets.




What You Bring

Proven experience in marketing, social media management, outbound marketing, lead generation, or related fields.
Demonstrated success growing audiences, increasing engagement, generating leads, or supporting business development efforts.
Ability to showcase previous campaigns, audience growth results, content performance, or measurable marketing outcomes.

Confidence speaking with U.S.-based business owners and decision-makers.

Experience creating content across multiple platforms and formats.
Familiarity with social media management and outbound email marketing.
Basic video editing and content formatting skills are preferred.
Strong organizational skills and exceptional attention to detail.
Ability to manage multiple priorities and execute independently.
Comfortable working in a fast-paced environment where initiative and follow-through matter.




Why This Role Matters

Every marketing campaign, social post, outreach message, and prospect conversation creates an opportunity for growth.

In this role, you'll help shape how our company is seen, how new clients discover us, and how opportunities enter our pipeline. Your work will directly contribute to brand visibility, lead generation, client engagement, and long-term business growth.




If you're excited about combining marketing, content creation, outreach, and relationship-building into a role where your work creates measurable impact, we'd love to hear from you.

description:
About the job

¡Muchas gracias por tu interés en querer formar parte de nuestra familia #GenteViva!

En Viva nuestro principal propósito es que la gente se divierta, haga negocio y se conecte con sus seres queridos aprovechando la reducción de costo en el transporte aéreo.

¡Estamos seguros que con tu talento, seguiremos volando cada vez más alto!

Como CRM & Email Marketing Analyst, serás responsable de diseñar, ejecutar y optimizar la estrategia de comunicación post booking desde CRM, con foco en la venta de ancillaries y servicios adicionales. Esta posición tiene como objetivo identificar oportunidades de negocio mediante el análisis de datos, segmentación de audiencias y diseño de iniciativas personalizadas que incrementen los ingresos complementarios y mejoren la experiencia del pasajero en su preparación para el viaje a través de los canales de CRM (email, push, in app, etc).

Funciones Principales


Diseñar journeys automatizados y campañas específicas para promover ancillaries (maletas, asientos, upgrades, etc.) y servicios complementarios después de la reserva.
Identificar tendencias, fricciones y oportunidades mediante el análisis de datos transaccionales y de comportamiento para optimizar la conversión de servicios adicionales.
Ejecutar experimentos para mejorar el performance de las campañas, identificando qué variables impactan más la conversión post booking.
Analiza datos de mercado, tendencias, comportamientos y recopila información de los clientes
Monitorear KPIs como revenue incremental, tasa de conversión por canal y por servicio, aperturas, clics y engagement, y proponer acciones con base en los hallazgos.
Crear y mantener segmentos dinámicos basados en perfil, historial, ruta, canal y tiempo hasta el vuelo para ofrecer mensajes relevantes y oportunos.


Requisitos y Habilidades


Licenciatura concluida en Marketing, Comunicación, Diseño, Mercadotecnia o carreras afines.
Experiencia de 2 a 6 años en CRM, Email Marketing y Marketing Automation.
Experiencia desarrollando estrategias de upselling, campañas automatizadas y customer journeys postcompra.
Conocimiento y manejo de herramientas de segmentación CDP (Customer Data Platform).
Experiencia utilizando herramientas de automatización.
Conocimiento de HTML para la edición y personalización de correos electrónicos.
Dominio avanzado de Microsoft Office, (Excel, Power Point, Word).
