description:
About the job

Company Introduction

WorldStrides is the global leader in educational travel and experiential learning. The company was founded in 1967 to provide middle school travel programs to Washington, D.C. and has grown to provide a wide range of programs for more than half a million students annually to over 100 countries around the world. WorldStrides offers experiential learning programs in educational travel, performing arts, language immersion, career exploration, service-learning, study abroad, and sports. Each of these experiences helps students to see beyond the classroom and to see the world – and themselves – in new ways.

Job Description

The Lead Generation Representative / Lead Qualifier is responsible for generating new business opportunities through high-volume outbound calls and email outreach to schools and teachers across the U.S.

In this role, you will: Proactively prospect and engage educators to introduce WorldStrides programs, qualify leads by identifying teachers interested in organizing student travel experiences, consistently meet and exceed monthly targets for calls and qualified leads, and collaborate with the Sales team by passing high-quality opportunities for closing.

This is a goal-driven, outbound sales role where success depends on your ability to handle rejection and stay motivated, build rapport quickly over the phone, maintain strong activity levels daily, and meet weekly and monthly goals in order to achieve additional compensation through a set commission structure.

You’ll work with a wide range of educational travel programs (history, science, national parks, and more), giving you constant opportunities to connect with new prospects and refine your sales and communication skills.

Responsibilities


Make calls and leave messages for teachers nationwide and use talk tracks for live calls.
Send follow-up emails.
Work on special projects, including periodic competitive calls, calls to unassigned schools, specialty schools and other telemarketing efforts.
Periodically research schools to help identify top prospects and maintain the database. Use templates to create and send flyers and update PowerPoint presentations.


Qualifications


Desired work experience in sales, marketing, promotions or telemarketing.
Proficiency in relevant computer applications including CRM.
Desired knowledge of customer service/sales principles and practices.
Six months or more experience in sales/lead creation/lead qualifying with Telephone sales experience preferred
Good data entry/keyboard skills
Familiar with office rules/office environment
High school diploma or GED required
Advanced (85%+) English level
Desired knowledge of sales and marketing principles and strategies
Proactive
Organized


Work Perks


Fun & driven environment.
Excellent medical, dental, and vision coverage, life, accidental death and dismemberment, accident, critical illness, and disability insurance.
+10% of gross salary as food stamps.
1 paid volunteer day & 25 PTO* days to start – accrue up to 28 over 3 years, 4 mental health days, and 5 bereavement days.
Statutory Holidays by Mexican Federal Labor Law.
Tuition reimbursement up to five thousand, two hundred fifty dollars annually or one thousand dollars towards professional certification annually.
Flexible work schedule providing on-site and hybrid opportunities.
Encouraged participation in our Employee Resource Groups and Diversity, Equity, & Inclusion council.
Fitness opportunities. Employee Assistance Program (EAP)
Paid statutory parental leave.


WorldStrides, a global organization, is committed to educate and serve communities worldwide. Our commitment is fueled by the passion of our team members and partners to make experiential learning accessible, while also being socially, environmentally, and ethically responsible. Together, we accomplish this by investing in initiatives to promote inclusion, diversity, and sustainability.

As an Equal Opportunity Employer, WorldStrides is committed to building a diverse workforce, supported by an environment that promotes inclusion and belonging. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, age, sex, national origin, disability status, genetics, protected veteran status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state, or local laws.

WorldStrides will only employ those who are legally authorized to work in the United States. This is not a position for which sponsorship will be provided. Individuals with temporary visas such as E, F-1, J-1, H-1, H-2, L, B, J or TN, or who need sponsorship for work authorization now or in the future, are not eligible for hire. Select seasonal roles may consider students on J-1 or F-1 visas.

title: ABM Specialist - Remote Work
description:
About the job

At BairesDev®, we've been leading the way in technology projects for over 15 years. We deliver cutting-edge solutions to giants like Google and the most innovative startups in Silicon Valley.




Our diverse 4,000+ team, composed of the world's Top 1% of tech talent, works remotely on roles that drive significant impact worldwide.




When you apply for this position, you're taking the first step in a process that goes beyond the ordinary. We aim to align your passions and skills with our vacancies, setting you on a path to exceptional career development and success.




ABM Specialist at BairesDev




In this role, you'll support BairesDev's ABM and demand-generation efforts to help fuel commercial growth. You'll help plan and execute campaigns for strategic accounts, working with Sales to support opportunities and expand client relationships. It's a great position for a marketer who values measurable impact and wants their campaigns to influence revenue.




What You'll Do:




- Help develop integrated ABM and demand-generation campaigns that target enterprise and tech accounts.

- Work with Sales and SDR teams to help identify and prioritize target accounts and buyer personas.

- Support multi-channel campaigns across digital, events, and outreach to drive engagement.

- Help measure campaign performance and optimize tactics to improve ROI and conversion rates.

- Support alignment between marketing and commercial teams to ensure smooth handoff of qualified opportunities.




What we are looking for:




- Experience in Account-Based Marketing or demand generation.

- Background in B2B marketing.

- Experience with email marketing campaigns.

- Good analytical and campaign management skills.

- Good level of English.




How we do make your work (and your life) easier:




- 100% remote work (from anywhere).

- Excellent compensation in USD or your local currency if preferred

- Hardware and software setup for you to work from home.

- Flexible hours: create your own schedule.

- Paid parental leaves, vacations, and holidays.

- Innovative and multicultural work environment: collaborate and learn from the global Top 1% of talent.

- Supportive environment with mentorship, promotions, skill development, and diverse growth opportunities.




Apply now and become part of a global team where your unique talents can truly thrive!

description:
About the job

Digital Marketing Content Specialist - Remote | Full-Time

About The Company

We are a private equity firm that owns and operates two regional lawn and pest control companies — one in New Orleans, Louisiana, and one in Jacksonville, Florida. Both businesses are in active growth mode with a concentrated push into digital marketing to drive customer acquisition and brand visibility across their local markets.

About The Role

This role exists because leadership has a clear marketing vision and no bandwidth to execute it. The principals are running these businesses day-to-day and can set the strategy — they need someone who can take a brief, work independently, and deliver finished product without being managed through every step.

You won't be handed a detailed playbook for every task. You'll be handed a direction and expected to move. This is an execution role, and the right person will treat it like a craft.

This person will support digital marketing efforts across both portfolio companies.

Key Responsibilities

Ad Content Production


Build Facebook and Instagram ad variations based on strategy briefs from leadership — expect to produce 5-10 creative variations per campaign
Write ad copy across multiple angles (offer-driven, testimonial, awareness, seasonal) and test messaging approaches
Resize and adapt existing creative assets for different placements and formats (feed, story, reel, carousel)
Monitor ad performance at a surface level and flag low performers or creative fatigue to leadership
Stay current on Meta ad best practices — formats, creative trends, and what's working in local service marketing


Video Editing & Production Support


Edit raw video footage into social-ready clips for Facebook, Instagram, and YouTube Shorts
Add captions, transitions, music, and branded elements to raw footage
Maintain brand consistency across all video output — tone, fonts, color treatment
Turn around edited video within 24 hours of receiving source material in most cases

Maintain a library of approved brand assets, fonts, colors, and logo files for both companies

Content Creation & Copywriting

Adapt tone and messaging between the two portfolio brands as needed
Use AI tools to accelerate content drafts — then refine, edit, and elevate the output to publish-ready quality


Workflow, Communication & Execution

Receive direction asynchronously and execute with minimal follow-up needed
Proactively flag blockers, capacity constraints, or timeline risks — before they become problems

Video editing competency in CapCut, DaVinci Resolve, Adobe Premiere, or equivalent
Graphic design skills sufficient for social and print assets (Canva, Adobe Express, Photoshop, or similar)
Strong written English — this person communicates directly with company principals and produces outward-facing content

Preferred Qualifications


Experience supporting multiple brands or clients simultaneously (agency background is a strong signal)
Familiarity with home services, trades, lawn care, pest control, or local service business marketing
Working knowledge of Meta Ads Manager — campaign structure, audience basics, creative specs
Experience managing or contributing to a content calendar across multiple accounts

description:
About the job

This position is listed on behalf of a partner company, who manages all applications and next steps. Our partner is looking for a Lifecycle & Retention Strategist at E-commerce Growth Agency based in Mexico.

This role sits at the intersection of strategy, data, and customer experience, focused on helping high-growth subscription e-commerce brands significantly improve customer lifetime value and long-term retention. You will act as a key strategic partner to clients, shaping lifecycle marketing programs that directly impact revenue, churn reduction, and subscriber engagement. The environment is fast-paced and highly analytical, with strong emphasis on experimentation, performance measurement, and continuous optimization. You will work across multiple client accounts, collaborating with specialists in CRM execution, design, and copywriting to bring retention strategies to life. This is a client-facing role requiring both strategic thinking and hands-on ownership of lifecycle marketing performance. Your work will directly influence how leading CPG subscription brands scale sustainably.

Accountabilities


Develop and execute lifecycle and retention strategies to maximize LTV and reduce churn across subscription-based brands
Own client relationships as the main strategic point of contact for retention marketing initiatives
Build and optimize lifecycle journeys including email/SMS automations, segmentation, and retention flows in tools like Klaviyo
Conduct cohort and customer behavior analyses to identify trends and retention opportunities
Manage retention calendars and campaign planning across multiple client accounts
Analyze performance metrics and provide actionable insights to improve retention outcomes
Collaborate with CRM specialists, designers, and copywriters to execute high-impact campaigns
Lead retention projects end-to-end, ensuring timely delivery and strong performance results
Continuously monitor industry trends in subscription commerce and lifecycle marketing to inform strategy
Strengthen long-term client relationships and contribute to account growth and retention


Requirements

This role requires strong experience in lifecycle or retention marketing within e-commerce or subscription-based business models, combined with a deep understanding of customer data, CRM systems, and performance-driven marketing. The ideal candidate is both strategic and hands-on, with strong client management skills and the ability to turn data into actionable growth strategies.


4+ years of experience in retention marketing, lifecycle marketing, CRM, or similar roles
Strong expertise in Klaviyo (flows, automations, segmentation, reporting, and lifecycle strategy)
Proven ability to conduct cohort analysis and derive insights from customer behavior data
Experience working with e-commerce, DTC, or subscription-based brands
Strong analytical mindset with the ability to translate data into strategic recommendations
Excellent client-facing communication and stakeholder management skills
Experience managing multiple accounts or projects in a fast-paced environment
Familiarity with subscription platforms such as ReCharge, Skio, or similar tools is a plus
Strong understanding of subscription commerce and customer lifecycle marketing principles
Comfortable using AI tools (e.g., ChatGPT, Claude) to improve productivity and workflow efficiency


Benefits


Fully remote working model with flexibility
Competitive salary package aligned with experience
Opportunity to work with fast-growing subscription-based e-commerce brands
Exposure to advanced lifecycle marketing and retention growth strategies
International, collaborative team environment with strong domain expertise
High ownership role with direct client impact and strategic influence
Long-term growth opportunities within a scaling agency

title: PPC Specialist (Lead Generation, Home Services)
description:
About the job

About company

We are a fast-growing performance agency focused on lead generation for home-services brands. We value ethics, clarity, and measurable outcomes. The team invests in learning and avoids siloed hand-offs so people can own results end to end.




The role

Run PPC for a portfolio of home-services clients and be accountable for outcomes from onboarding through scale. The job blends in-platform execution with tagging, analytics, and client communication. You will work alongside teammates in data and client services rather than sitting in a narrow channel lane.




What you’ll do

• Plan, build, launch, and optimize Google Ads and Microsoft Ads for lead generation

• Join client calls, translate data into decisions, set expectations, and build trust

• Own measurement quality with Google Tag Manager, including data layer checks and debugging

• Use GA4, CRM-based attribution views, Looker Studio, and CallRail to assess performance and recommend changes

• Maintain hygiene, budgets, and a steady test plan; use scripts where helpful

• Collaborate with the existing account team during onboarding and share context with peers




What you’ll bring

• 3-4 or more years in PPC for lead generation, with solid home-services experience

• Strong English for a client-facing role; accents are welcome

• Hands-on strength in Google Ads or Microsoft Ads plus GTM, GA4, Sheets, Looker Studio, and basic CallRail usage

• Comfort reading closed-loop data and making decisions from CRM-sourced revenue

• Clear thinking, attention to detail, and the ability to move between strategy and execution

• Ability to align most working hours to the Central time U.S. day; typical weeks average 40 to 50 hours




Nice to have

• Experience with Google Ads scripts or simple automation

• Zapier or similar workflow tools

• Familiarity with Google Ads API concepts and partnering with data engineers

• Landing page tools such as WordPress, Unbounce, or Leadpages




Interview process

• Initial interview

• Paid audit task

• Follow-up interview to review your work

• Decision




Start date

As soon as possible, with respect for notice periods.




Schedule: Align most of your day to the U.S. Central work window; plan to overlap broadly between 7:00 a.m. and 7:00 p.m. CT

description:
About the job

EL EQUIPO

El equipo de Universe forma parte del ecosistema de Ticketmaster y está enfocado en ofrecer soluciones innovadoras de venta de boletos para eventos de admisión general, complementadas con estrategias de marketing digital que impulsan el alcance, engagement y conversión de nuestros clientes.




DESCRIPCIÓN DEL CARGO

Buscamos un Ejecutivo/a de Servicios Digitales para Universe, nuestra plataforma enfocada en eventos de admisión general, que combine la venta de boletos con soluciones estratégicas de marketing digital. Esta posición será responsable de asesorar a clientes (promotores, venues y organizadores) para maximizar el desempeño de sus eventos a través de herramientas digitales y acompañamiento consultivo.




Tendrás la oportunidad de trabajar en un puesto que combina tecnología y marketing digital impactando directamente en la venta de boletos y en la experiencia de nuestros clientes.







LO QUE HARÁS

Asesorar a clientes sobre el uso las soluciones de marketing digital de para maximizar la venta de boletos.
Ejecutar reuniones consultivas con organizadores e inmuebles para entender sus necesidades y proponer estrategias digitales.
Coordinar la implementación de campañas digitales (orgánicas y paid media) para eventos en la plataforma.
Gestionar la recepción de contenidos asegurando el cumplimiento de lineamientos técnicos y creativos.
Dar seguimiento a la ejecución de campañas para asegurar entregables en tiempo y forma.
Monitorear métricas clave de desempeño para optimizar resultados comerciales.
Elaborar y presentar reportes de resultados a clientes utilizando herramientas analíticas.
Coordinar la publicación de contenido en canales digitales como blog y LinkedIn B2B para posicionamiento de marca.
Documentar acuerdos, minutas y planes de acción para garantizar el seguimiento con clientes y equipos internos.
Colaborar con equipos internos (Paid Media, Comercial, Revenue, Legal, Operaciones) para la correcta ejecución de campañas.
Proponer mejoras continuas en estrategias digitales basadas en análisis de datos y tendencias de mercado.
Fortalecer relaciones con clientes para asegurar retención y crecimiento.




LO QUE NECESITAS SABER (o HABILIDADES TÉCNICAS)

Licenciatura en Marketing, Publicidad, Comunicación o afín.
Experiencia mínima de 2 años en marketing digital o gestión de campañas.
Conocimiento en marketing B2B y manejo de cuentas o clientes.
Experiencia en interpretación de métricas digitales y analítica.
Manejo de herramientas como Office 365; deseable Google Analytics y Looker Studio.
Experiencia en coordinación de campañas paid media y orgánicas.
Inglés intermedio-avanzado.
Habilidad para gestionar múltiples proyectos simultáneamente.
Conocimiento en plataformas digitales, e-commerce




TÚ (BEHAVIOURAL SKILLS)

Construyes relaciones de confianza a largo plazo con clientes y stakeholders.
Comunicas ideas de forma clara, empática y efectiva en distintos niveles de la organización.
Te enfocas en cumplir y superar objetivos comerciales.
Comunicas ideas de forma clara, estructurada y persuasiva.
Tomas decisiones basadas en datos y análisis.
Te adaptas a entornos dinámicos y de rápido crecimiento.
Actúas con ética y responsabilidad en todas tus interacciones.




VIDA EN TICKETMASTER

Nos enorgullece formar parte de Live Nation Entertainment, la empresa de entretenimiento en vivo más grande del mundo. Nuestra visión en Ticketmaster es conectar a las personas alrededor del mundo con los eventos en vivo que aman.

Como el mercado de boletos más grande del mundo y el proveedor global líder de herramientas y servicios para la industria del entretenimiento en vivo, estamos en una posición única para cumplir con esa visión.

Lo hacemos todo con una pasión intensa por el entretenimiento en vivo y una cultura inspiradora y diversa, impulsada por líderes accesibles, gerentes atentos y equipos entusiastas. Si te apasiona el entretenimiento en vivo como a nosotros, y deseas trabajar en una empresa dedicada a ayudar a millones de fanáticos a vivir esa experiencia, queremos saber de ti.

Nuestra labor se guía por nuestros valores:

Fiabilidad: Entendemos que los fanáticos y clientes dependen de nosotros para hacer posible sus experiencias en eventos en vivo, y nosotros dependemos los unos de los otros para lograrlo.
Trabajo en equipo: Creemos que el logro individual es insignificante en comparación con el éxito que se puede lograr en equipo.
Integridad: Estamos comprometidos con los más altos estándares morales y éticos en nombre de los innumerables socios y partes interesadas que representamos.
Sentido de pertenencia: Estamos comprometidos a construir una cultura en la que todas las personas puedan ser su yo auténtico, tengan voz igualitaria y oportunidades para prosperar.







OPORTUNIDADES IGUALES

Estamos apasionados y comprometidos con nuestra gente, y vamos más allá de la retórica sobre diversidad e inclusión. Trabajarás en un entorno inclusivo y se te alentará a ser tú mismo en el trabajo. Haremos todo lo posible para ayudarte a equilibrar exitosamente tu vida laboral y personal. Como negocio en crecimiento, fomentaremos que desarrolles tanto tus aspiraciones profesionales como personales, disfrutes de nuevas experiencias y aprendas de las personas talentosas con las que trabajarás. Nos importa el talento, y alentamos las postulaciones de personas sin importar su género, raza, orientación sexual, religión, edad, estado de discapacidad o responsabilidades familiares.




PRÁCTICAS DE CONTRATACIÓN

La descripción del puesto anterior ha sido diseñada para indicar la naturaleza general y el nivel del trabajo realizado por los empleados dentro de esta clasificación. No está destinada a contener ni a interpretarse como un inventario exhaustivo de todas las funciones, responsabilidades y cualificaciones requeridas para los empleados asignados a este puesto.

Las políticas de contratación de Ticketmaster están diseñadas para incorporar a las personas más calificadas disponibles de manera oportuna y eficiente. Ticketmaster puede utilizar todos los medios disponibles, incluyendo la promoción interna, referencias de empleados, publicidad externa, agencias de empleo, reclutamiento por internet, ferias de empleo, reclutamiento universitario y head hunters. Ticketmaster puede utilizar herramientas de inteligencia artificial (IA) para apoyar la preselección y evaluación de candidaturas. Todas las decisiones de contratación se toman con revisión humana.

description:
About the job

Email Marketing Specialist (Design Skill Required)




Work Schedule: Full-Time (40 Hours/Week) | Flexible working time zone.

Location: Remote

Type: Full-Time / Long-Term Contract




Company Overview

Our client’s company is a founder-led venture studio specializing in scaling health and wellness brands, they are hiring an experienced Email Marketing Specialist to lead lifecycle marketing across multiple brands.




The studio works closely with founders from product inception through global expansion, providing hands-on support across brand development, e-commerce, marketing, and growth strategy. Their team partners with wellness brands to build high-performing marketing systems designed for long-term scalability.




Role Overview

We are looking for a highly experienced Email Marketing Specialist who can take ownership of email strategy across multiple e-commerce brands while managing a small team responsible for execution.




This role requires someone who is both strategic and operational — capable of planning lifecycle marketing strategies, optimizing revenue-driving flows, managing campaign calendars, and ensuring all emails meet high standards of performance and quality.




Key Responsibilities

Manage email marketing strategy across multiple e-commerce brands
Build and optimize lifecycle flows (Welcome, Abandoned Cart, Post-Purchase, Replenishment, Win-back)
Plan and manage weekly campaign calendars
Oversee a small team responsible for email design, copy, and execution
Analyze campaign and flow performance to improve revenue
Design or edit email templates and visual assets using Figma or similar tools, ensuring brand consistency across campaigns
Run A/B tests and optimize segmentation and targeting
Collaborate with marketing and design teams to execute campaigns and maintain high-quality standards.




Requirements

+5 years managing email marketing for e-commerce brands
Proven experience managing multiple brands or accounts simultaneously
Hands-on experience with Klaviyo or similar email marketing platforms
Working knowledge of Figma or similar design tools to create or edit email templates and visual assets
Strong understanding of lifecycle marketing and automated flows
Experience managing or coordinating designers, copywriters, or marketing contractors
Excellent English communication skills
Strong organizational and project management skills
Ability to manage campaign calendars and prioritize multiple projects
Experience working with high-growth consumer brands
Understanding of SMS marketing strategies
Advanced knowledge of segmentation and personalization
Experience scaling email revenue as a major channel for e-commerce brands




The ideal candidate is someone who:

Is both strategic and detail-oriented
Has a strong understanding of e-commerce lifecycle marketing
Can manage both execution and team leadership
Thrives in fast-paced environments managing multiple brands
Is highly data-driven and focused on revenue growth

About the job

About the Role

We are looking for a PO Follow-Up Analyst to join our Procurement Shared Services (PSS) team. This is a newly created role that plays a key part in ensuring accurate purchase order status, supplier follow-up, and seamless communication between internal teams and external partners across the region.

This position supports the Regional Front Office and local PSS, contributing to a highly centralized and efficient procurement operating model.

Key Responsibilities

PO Follow-Up & Confirmation

Monitor PO status through Supplier Portal reports, including past-due deliveries and supplier confirmations
Review PO status with buyers and internal stakeholders when required
Contact suppliers to obtain updates and expedite deliveries
Ensure the Supplier Portal is accurate, complete, and up to date
Communicate PO status clearly to all relevant stakeholders

General Activities

Manage requests via Ticketing Tools, coordinating with requestors, buyers, and suppliers
Handle Credit Hold issues, PTE, Obsolete Materials, and Resource processes

Business Context

This role supports the transformation of the Procurement function toward a more centralized regional model. The PO Follow-Up Specialist is critical in maintaining high service levels, operational efficiency, and stakeholder satisfaction while managing Maintenance and Safety Supplies purchase orders.

Scope of the Role

Follow-up of material purchase orders (Maintenance & Safety Supplies)
Monitoring PO status, deliveries, and supplier confirmations
Acting as the main point of contact between internal users, Procurement teams, and suppliers

Key Stakeholders

Sourcing Teams
Category Managers
Suppliers
PTP Teams
ABI Facilities & Storerooms

Qualifications & Skills

Required

University degree or equivalent (Administration or similar)
1–2 years of experience in Procurement, Supply Chain, Purchasing, or PO Management roles
Strong understanding of sourcing tools (SAP)
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
Ability to translate data into business-impact insights
Advanced conversational English is mandatory (daily interaction with regional and global stakeholders)
Excellent communication skills with the ability to convey information clearly, request inputs, and engage in open, candid conversations
Strong collaboration and stakeholder management skills

description:
About the job

Sobre la oportunidad

Estamos construyendo un Equipo de Ventas de Campo con el objetivo principal de incrementar la oferta en distintas ciudades de México.

El role de Field Sales Representative es clave: será los ojos y oídos de Uber en el mercado. Su misión será asegurar que la oferta responda a la demanda, conectando proactivamente con socios conductores potenciales a través de visitas en campo y llamadas dirigidas, comunicando de forma efectiva la propuesta de valor de Uber.

Responsabilidades


 Identificar y encontrar prospectos (conductores de taxi o independientes) directamente en campo (80% del tiempo) para referirlos y/o convertirlos a la plataforma. 
 Actuar como la voz del socio conductor, capturando información de valor en cada interacción.

 Capacidad para operar con resiliencia, persistencia y enfoque ante la ambigüedad o metas de conversión desafiantes. 
 Disponibilidad para viajar dentro del país para apoyar campañas estratégicas de oferta y lanzamientos de nuevos mercados. 


Cualidades preferidas


 Conocimiento o experiencia laboral en la industria de taxis, transporte o logística en México.

We are hiring for one of our clients, seeking a Web Browsing Evaluator to work on a contract basis. Evaluators will follow detailed guidelines to provide feedback on search engine performance and user interface effectiveness.

Equal Opportunity Employer:

description:
About the job

A little bit about us:




We are Belvo, an open finance API platform with the bold vision of democratizing access to financial services in Latin America. We enable any financial innovator to access and interpret financial data, as well as initiate payments from their end-users accounts. We’re turning the messy complexities of the Latin American financial ecosystem into a modern set of tools to access and interpret data and move money in a seamless and secure way.

We’re a highly-technical, passionate, and driven team. We are more than 90 people and our team currently represents 20 nationalities. We have offices in São Paulo and Mexico City – while a large portion of us work remotely.

We are tackling a very stimulating problem: connecting fintech innovators with legacy financial infrastructure. We strive to go beyond the limits of what is possible today and to do so in an elegant and developer-first way.







About the team:




We work in cross-functional, autonomous teams. We follow continuous delivery best practices executed on top of a modern technology stack. 
Our products are built for developers, by developers. Technological excellence is at the heart of what we do. 
We are pragmatic and customer-focused. We strive to find the right set of trade-offs in order to validate our hypothesis as early as possible, iterating on our products based on customer feedback. 
We communicate transparently. We do weekly all-hands where we get together to discuss company performance and goals.
While we are global and remote-friendly, we also operate from our vibrant offices in CDMX and Sao Paulo. To accommodate the various time zones in which we are based, we ensure we’re always synced up for at least 3 hours per day.
Also, we are backed by some of the leading investors in Silicon Valley and Latin America, including Founders Fund, Kaszek Ventures, and YCombinator.




Your opportunity:




As a Talent Acquisition & People Operations Specialist at Belvo, you will play a key role in attracting and hiring top talent across multiple functions - partnering closely with hiring managers and leadership to understand hiring needs, build sourcing strategies, and manage recruitment processes end-to-end.

We're looking for someone who genuinely loves recruitment and cares deeply about people - and wants real ownership over their work in a team where culture isn't a slide deck, it's how we operate every day. Our People team is distributed, highly hands-on, and driven by culture. We believe the best companies are built on great hires, and that accuracy and an excellent employee experience are non-negotiables.




Talent Acquisition:




Source, screen, and hire the best talent across LATAM - tech, product, business, and beyond.
Own the full recruiting cycle, from job posting and sourcing to offer.

Exemplify a level of assertiveness to identify issues and solve them.
Introduction into data entry – Load building and scheduling
Gain knowledge of the transportation industry to better serve our customers,carriers, and drivers.

Qualifications

High school Diploma

Ability to work in a fast pace environment
Proactiveness

About the job

Introduction

At IBM Infrastructure & Technology, we design and operate the systems that keep the world running. From high-resiliency mainframes and hybrid cloud platforms to networking, automation, and site reliability. Our teams ensure the performance, security, and scalability that clients and industries depend on every day. Working in Infrastructure & Technology means tackling complex challenges with curiosity and collaboration. You’ll work with diverse technologies and colleagues worldwide to deliver resilient, future-ready solutions that power innovation. With continuous learning, career growth, and a supportive culture, IBM provides the opportunities to build expertise and shape the infrastructure that drives progress.

Your Role And Responsibilities

As a Supply Chain Professional, you will be responsible for applied real-time end-to-end Supply Chain management and leadership expertise on a global, multi-channel, multi-brand, and multi-partner basis. You will execute strategies to optimize cost and inventory to support the planning, building, and coordination of delivery of our products to our clients.

Your Primary Responsibilities Will Include


 Execute Supply Chain Strategies: Deliver real-time end-to-end Supply Chain management, collaborating with global logistics, procurement, and other executive teams to optimize the supply network.

 Leverage Advanced Technologies: Apply advanced technologies such as Artificial Intelligence, Command Center, and Cognitive Supply Chain Advisor (CSCA) 360 to enhance supply chain operations.

About the job

Overview

CAREERS TO SMILE ABOUT

At PepsiCo, you’ll discover a place where our mission is to create smiles around the world. With a portfolio of more than 500 beloved brands including, Gatorade, Lay’s and Quaker, our work touches millions of people every day.

At the heart of the company is a team of thinkers, creators, and problem-solvers who collaborate to innovate and turn ideas into action. Driven by innovation and a focus on creating joyful moments through food and drinks, our decisions are guided by consumer centricity, creating opportunities for our associates to do meaningful work and make a lasting impact in the communities we serve.

Whatever your role, you’ll be part of a global community that values your ideas and empowers you to make an impact, on your career and on the world around you.

Responsibilities

The Opportunity

This position contributes to the success of the Pepsi Beverages Company by providing outstanding customer service to customers and employees in the California, Mountain and Midwest Market Units. The agent will answer inbound calls and ensure issues are resolved through the screening and tracking of each request and dispatching to the appropriate Pepsi service team; while also performing follow up on each call to ensure complete resolution.

Your Impact

Answer and provide outstanding customer service of 50+ calls a day (Based on avg call volume of 500 calls per day)
Process inbound calls to determine appropriate area, (i.e., Delivery, Merchandising, MEM, etc.)
Partner with other departments to achieve root cause resolution of customer issues and provide feedback through reporting.
Achieve individual and team objectives to include: Average Call Handle Time, Calls per Hour, Cases Closed, Quality Service Level, Achieve < 2% abandonment rate goal for the Pepsi Support Center
Maintain complete and accurate documentation within the Customer Service Center Call Log

Contribute toward team goals and objectives

Communicate with field partners to ensure up-to-date staffing/location contact changes
